codeReview.summary: Documentation-only feature. Worker created comprehensive 355-line FACTORY-DROID.md documenting Factory Droid integration via .factory/services.yaml. The services.yaml was already correctly configured (from foundation-repo-setup). Worker verified YAML validity and accurately documented all service fields, integration patterns, prerequisites, troubleshooting, and architecture.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker created documentation in docs/FACTORY-DROID.md following the pattern of existing docs (OPENCODE.md, OH-MY-PI.md). This is consistent with the mission's documentation structure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: docs/FACTORY-DROID.md, docs/OPENCODE.md, docs/OH-MY-PI.md all follow similar format

sharedStateObservations.observation: The stop command in services.yaml uses broad pkill patterns ('pkill -f "bun run proxy"') which could match unintended processes. This was already present before this feature and is not introduced by the worker.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: services.yaml stop: 'pkill -f "bun run proxy" || pkill -f "standalone-server" || true'

summary: Pass. Feature correctly documents Factory Droid integration via services.yaml. The 355-line FACTORY-DROID.md comprehensively covers service manifest format, lifecycle management, health checking, integration with Factory Droid workflows, prerequisites (bun, cursor-agent), verification steps, troubleshooting, and architecture diagram. Worker verified services.yaml is valid YAML. All expected behaviors are addressed: Factory Droid can start proxy via services.yaml, proxy is accessible via health endpoint, and model access via chat completions is documented.
